meta.notes: Static Maquette landing page for waft, implemented under .maquette/pages/landing without modifying root site files.

sections.purpose: Provide persistent documentation navigation, theme toggle, source action, and mobile menu behavior.

layout.notes: Sticky nav with desktop inline links and tablet/mobile menu panel.

sections.notes: Text brand only; no generated logo is used.

sections.purpose: Introduce waft, show the primary command surface, and explain navigation behavior.

layout.notes: Hero copy and command block pair with inline SVG file-transfer art and nav behavior rail.

sections.notes: Introduces a page-local decorative file-transfer motif from inline SVG/CSS, not a reusable component or raster asset.

sections.purpose: Surface quick start, .worktreeinclude format, and safety guarantees in a compact three-column documentation band.

layout.notes: Columns collapse to a stack on tablet/mobile.

sections.notes: Uses page-local layout wrappers around existing component anatomy.

sections.purpose: Compare claude, git, and wt profile modes in equal-height cards with aligned command footers.
sections.notes: Card footers use component bottom-pinning behavior.

sections.purpose: Close with product blurb, docs/resource/community links, diagnostic command block, and air-current strip.

layout.notes: Dark terminal-style footer stacks on mobile.

sections.notes: Introduces page-local terminal footer composition using existing command/link primitives.

new_composites.name: file-transfer-motif
new_composites.purpose: Decorative inline SVG representation of ignored files wafting from main to feature.
new_composites.notes: Page-local and decorative; not a logo, mark, or reusable component.

new_composites.name: terminal-footer-composition
new_composites.purpose: Combine footer link columns, brand blurb, command block, and air-current strip.
new_composites.notes: Built from existing footer, link, and command primitives.

acceptance_criteria: All Maquette-owned page artifacts stay under .maquette/pages/landing.
Page reuses approved component CSS and JS before page-specific CSS.
Hero, usage, format, safety, profiles, and footer regions from the concept are represented.
Primary navigation has accessible desktop, collapsed mobile, and expanded mobile states.
Command lines and code examples wrap without forcing page-level horizontal overflow.
Profile cards use equal-height behavior and bottom-pinned command/action footers.
Footer remains a specific terminal-style composition rather than a generic link list.
No generated logo, mascot, app icon, seal, or brand mark is introduced.
Light and dark mode use approved token behavior.

quality_review.font_rationale: Page uses the approved Inter/system UI and JetBrains Mono/ui-monospace token stacks; no novelty or Impact-style fonts are used.
quality_review.concept_region_inventory: pages/landing/concept-region-inventory.md lists every visible concept region and implementation status.
quality_review.page_layout_contract: pages/landing/page-layout-contract.md records section density, footer structure, and responsive behavior before implementation.
quality_review.asset_manifest: pages/landing/asset-manifest.json records the concept screenshot and code-rendered decorative motif.
quality_review.component_sheet_gallery_fidelity: Page consumes the approved component catalog and CSS/JS files; component screenshot QA remains blocked.
quality_review.repeated_card_alignment: Profile cards use component doc-card grid and margin-top:auto footers for aligned command rows.
quality_review.footer_fidelity: Footer implements brand blurb, link columns, diagnostic command block, and bottom air-current strip from the concept.
quality_review.mobile_drawer_scrollability: Nav panel uses component max-block-size and overflow-y:auto behavior from the component library.
quality_review.social_icon_strategy: No social icon set is required by the final page; GitHub/source action uses an inline accessible icon.
quality_review.notes: Automated screenshot and measured responsive QA are blocked by unavailable optional project-local dependencies.
